what_it_does: An acceptance-test script that exercises turning a comment at a ui:// address into a surfaced build-intent. It verifies the surfaced intent's scope equals the S3 resolve_scope output, that orphan addresses produce an empty deny-all scope, that operator approval resolves without auto-dispatch, that ingest_comment leaves annotations and a gold chat turn, that surface_build_intent behavior is unchanged, that malformed/empty input is rejected, and that a bridge route exposes the feedback-to-intent path while approval stays on /api/resolve.
observations: Self-contained script with a long spec docstring and section markers.
Defines a global PASS counter and a custom check(label, cond) assertion helper.
Uses real corpus fixtures: ui://chat/input, ui://workshop/self-changes, ui://nonexistent/thing.
The central assertion compares the surfaced intent's scope to suite.resolve_scope(addr)['scope'].
Checks orphan intent has an empty scope and _in_any_scope returns False for arbitrary paths.
Verifies operator approval via resolve_surfaced emits a resolve event but no decision.dispatch event.
Verifies annotations_at and a located gold chat turn exist after surfacing.
Verifies bare surface_build_intent still produces an empty-scope, unresolved build intent.
Rejects malformed addresses and empty comment text by catching ValueError/KeyError.
Inspects runtime/bridge.py source as text to confirm a route and /api/resolve usage.
standouts: The 'one load-bearing assertion' design: surfaced scope must exactly equal resolve_scope output.
Explicit L1/L2 boundary: surfacing is L1, dispatch-on-approve is L2 and must not fire.
Use of live corpus addresses as fixtures instead of mocks or synthetic paths.
conventions: Heavy narrative docstring that doubles as a specification.
Plain-script acceptance test rather than a unittest/pytest class.
Uses tempfile for an isolated FsStore per run.
concerns: Reads bridge.py as raw source and checks string presence, not runtime behavior.
Calls the private suite._in_any_scope directly from an acceptance test.
Fixture scopes are tied to exact file paths that can drift with corpus changes.
Catches ValueError/KeyError broadly for both malformed addresses and empty comments.
notes: A narrative acceptance test that locks down the addressed-feedback-to-build-intent contract with real corpus fixtures and a strict no-auto-dispatch boundary.
questions: What is the exact implementation of Suite.surface_intent_at and how does it call ingest_comment and surface_build_intent?
How does resolve_surfaced differ from other resolution paths?
What is the I6 ingest_comment path and where is it implemented?
What does the /api/resolve route do and how is it protected as operator-only?
How are the real corpus addresses regenerated and kept in sync with these fixtures?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it is an L1 acceptance test for addressed feedback becoming a scoped, operator-approvable build-intent, with sections that map directly to the docstring claims.
apparent_themes: Scope fidelity and no fabricated broad scopes.
Layer separation between surfacing and dispatch.
Reuse of existing components over duplication.
intention_signals: It tries to prove that a comment at a UI address can be safely converted into a live build-intent whose scope is exactly what the corpus resolver says, while preserving existing safety boundaries and leaving dispatch to a later layer.
novelty: Treats the acceptance test as a layered contract narrative, explicitly marking L1 as 'surface only' and using a single scope-equality assertion as the load-bearing happy-path proof.
contribution: A concrete acceptance contract for feedback-to-build-intent surfacing, including scope-fidelity checks, orphan deny-all behavior, operator-only resolution without dispatch, input validation, and live corpus fixture addresses with expected scopes.
summary_for_embedding: An acceptance test script that validates a comment at a ui:// address becomes a scoped, unresolved build-intent via reused ingest_comment, resolve_scope, and surface_build_intent paths, with operator-only approval and no auto-dispatch.
intention_for_embedding: To lock down the L1 addressed-feedback-to-wire behavior and its safety boundaries as a runnable acceptance contract.

symbols.does: Test assertion helper that asserts a condition and increments a global PASS counter.
symbols.description: check(label, cond) is a minimal assertion helper used throughout the script. It asserts the supplied condition, increments the global PASS counter on success, and prints an 'ok' line with the given label; on failure it raises an AssertionError prefixed with 'FAIL: ' and the label.
